arts.ttl: McTominay hoping Scotland get better of fine margins with Greece tie in balance
arts.sum: Midfielder says criticism of Clarke ‘unjustified’ before Greece’s visit in the Nations League playoff second leg The scale of anger as Scotland were unceremoniously bundled out of Euro 2024 was such that there was cause to ponder whether Steve Clarke could or should survive as the manager. Clarke’s response has fully justified his remaining in post; Scotland take a narrow lead into the second leg of the Nations League playoff against Greece, with the retaining of status in the top tier within touching distance. From lying on the canvas, the Scots are unbeaten in four and have won three in a row. This feels like a team on an upward curve once more. Scott McTominay, the goalscorer in Greece, has made plain that questioning Clarke was folly. “In the summer it was very fine margins,” McTominay said. “The media will always say it is doom and gloom, stuff like that. It was such slim margins where, if you don’t take your moments in both boxes against top teams, you are going to get punished. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: The 'pretty special' 17-year-old out to dash Scotland dreams
arts.sum: Scotland take a hard-earned 1-0 lead into Sunday's second leg against Greece at Hampden Park, but no-one in their ranks is taking overall victory for granted, especially with the threat of a 'pretty special' 17-year-old in Konstantinos Karetsas.

arts.ttl: Steve Clarke challenges Scotland to lay down World Cup 'marker' as boss sees long term plan come to fruition
arts.sum: The Scotland boss is confident his team can finish the job in what is an 'important' game against Greece

arts.ttl: Che Adams makes McTominay penalty admission with Hampden vow caveat
arts.sum: Che Adams has no hard feelings towards Scott McTominay after he took the penalty to down Greece in the Nations League. But the former Manchester United midfielder took the ball and slotted home to give Steve Clarke’s side a massive first-leg lead in the Nations League play-off.

arts.ttl: Vladimir Romanov claims he blew Celtic out the water with 'blank cheque' he handed to amazed Craig Gordon
arts.sum: Romanov claims he offered Craig Gordon whatever he wanted to stay at Hearts – literally

arts.ttl: Steve Clarke explains Kieran Tierney snub for Greece clash but Brendan Rodgers and Celtic may have something else to say
arts.sum: Tierney was left on the bench to face Greece but what does it mean for his long-term international prospects?
